sections.paragraph:
ἀσφαλής, ές (s. ἀσφάλεια; Hom. et al.; Epict., ins, pap, LXX, TestSol, Philo, Joseph., Just.; Ath., R.)

① pert. to being stable, firm ἄγκυρα Hb 6:19 (w. βέβαιος, as Cebes 18, 3; 31, 1; Sext. Emp., Adv. Math. 8, 374; BGU 419, 18; Wsd 7:23; Dio Chrys. 34, 17 and 37; cp. 33, 17). τὸν ἀ. θεμέλιον the sure foundation 1 Cl 33:3 (cp. Wsd 4:3).

② fig. (Philo, Exs. 153; Jos., Bell. 2, 524) pert. to expression that ensures certainty about someth., certain, ἀσφαλές τι γράφειν write someth. definite Ac 25:26; τὸ ἀ. the certainty = the truth (in ref. to ferreting out the facts; cp. Mitt-Wilck, I/2, 17, 8 [Traj.] ἵνα τὸ ἀ. ἐπιγνῶ) γνῶναι 21:34; 22:30; ἡ ἀ. γνῶσις 1 Cl 1:2; Dg 12:4.

③ pert. to being in someone’s best interest, safe, secure (Demosth. 10, 70 βίος ἀ.; Jos., Ant. 3, 41 ἀ. καὶ σῶφρον=the safest and wisest) ὑμῖν (ἐστιν) ἀσφαλές it is (a) safe (course) for you Phil 3:1. ἀσφαλὲς εἷναι ISm 8:2.—B. 756; 1237. DELG s.v. σφάλλω. M-M. TW. Spicq.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Cebes Cebes , prominent in Platonic dialogues; ref. is to a dialogue associated with his name, I a.d.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 147
